summary: Frontend E2E testing of birthday puzzle multiplayer flow against live Firebase Realtime DB. Login, lobby (with 5min timer + player grid + start-now + revoke-cancel), atomic unique number assignment (P1=#01, P2=#02), real-time multi-player sync across 2 browser contexts, revoke-confirm (clears room), Start now -> /puzzle routing by assignedNumber, Puzzle1 (password 090503) solve, Waiting page progress bar, and Reveal page (single-player end-to-end seen with fragment '01 - Happy birthday.'). One bug found in revoke fan-out: only the player who triggered revoke is redirected; other already-joined players remain stuck on a stale /lobby route and are NOT auto-redirected to login.

integration_issues.issue: When player A clicks Revoke -> 'Yes, revoke', the room state and players are wiped in Firebase and player A is redirected to '/'. However other already-joined players (B, C ...) stay on /lobby with stale UI (their lobby renders 'empty seats' for everyone but localStorage 'bp_playerId' still points at the deleted player). They are not auto-redirected to login, and if they try to interact further, they are effectively orphaned. Reproduce: open 2 browser contexts, both join, in context #1 click Revoke -> Yes, revoke. Context #1 lands on '/'; context #2 stays on '/lobby'. Expected: context #2 should also detect the room reset (e.g. their playerId no longer exists under rooms/birthday-room/players) and redirect to '/' (clear localStorage bp_playerId).

passed_tests: Lobby shows 5-min countdown timer (data-testid=lobby-timer) and player grid (data-testid=player-card-1..12)
Multi-player real-time sync: both contexts see player-card-1 (Alice) and player-card-2 (Bob) live
Atomic unique assignedNumbers via runTransaction: P1=#01 Alice, P2=#02 Bob (no collision)
Revoke confirmation dialog (data-testid=revoke-confirm-dialog) appears on click
Revoke 'Never mind' (data-testid=revoke-cancel) closes dialog without state change; player remains in /lobby
Start now button (data-testid=start-now-btn) routes BOTH connected players to /puzzle simultaneously
Puzzle routing by assignedNumber: P1->Puzzle1Password (puzzle1-input), P2->Puzzle2Caesar (puzzle2-input)
Puzzle1 solve: filling '090503' and clicking unlock (puzzle1-submit) advances player out of /puzzle
Waiting page (data-testid=waiting-progress) shown while other players still solving
Single-player end-to-end: solve puzzle1 -> /reveal showing ordered fragment '01 Happy birthday.' with 'read by Solo - your turn'
Revoke confirm clears Firebase state and redirects the triggering player to '/'
action_items: In Lobby.jsx (or a top-level Firebase listener), add logic so that when a player's own playerId is no longer present under rooms/birthday-room/players (or counter resets to 0/state cleared), the client clears localStorage bp_playerId and navigates to '/'. Today this only happens for the player who clicked Revoke; other players stay on a stale /lobby.
critical_code_review_comments: Revoke flow is not idempotent across other clients: only the initiator's local state is cleared. Recommend listening to player presence and triggering navigate('/') when the local playerId disappears.
Firebase DB rules are wide-open ('.read': true, '.write': true) per PRD. Acceptable for test, MUST be tightened before any public deploy.
localStorage 'bp_playerId' persistence is intentional but creates orphan-player risk after revoke; adding the listener above also fixes that.

success_rate.frontend: 92% (12/13 scenarios pass; 1 multi-player revoke fan-out bug)

test_credentials: n/a (no auth) - players just enter a name
seed_data_creation: Used Firebase REST DELETE on rooms/birthday-room.json before each scenario to reset state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Live Firebase project birthday-nisha is reachable and behaving. Reset state between runs via: curl -X DELETE 'https://birthday-nisha-default-rtdb.asia-southeast1.firebasedatabase.app/rooms/birthday-room.json'. Login input has no type attribute, so use selector 'input' (not 'input[type=text]'). Single-player Start-now -> Puzzle1 -> Reveal flow is fully working. Multi-player atomic numbering and start-now broadcast also work. The remaining gap is revoke fan-out to other clients.
